security.title: Travel Safety in Ecuador
security.subtitle: Ecuador is a safe and welcoming destination. Here are key tips for a worry-free journey.

transport.title: Transport Security
transport.body: Tourism vehicles in Ecuador carry a special identification issued by the National Transit Agency (ANT). All road users across Ecuador are covered by free accident insurance throughout the national road network.

tips.title: General Recommendations
items: Keep in regular contact with family and friends.
Always leave valuables in the hotel safe.
Be cautious in crowded places, bars or nightclubs — never accept drinks from strangers.
For information, ask at your hotel reception or trusted tour operators. Avoid consulting strangers.

atm.title: ATMs & Cash
items: Exchange money only at authorized banks and exchange houses.
Go to the bank or ATM accompanied when possible.
Use ATMs in public, well-lit locations.
Do not accept help or company from unknown people.
Keep your documents and cards in sight when making payments.

police.title: Tourist Police Unit
police.body: The National Community & Tourism Police Unit carries out preventive and operational activities for national and foreign tourists. Officers are deployed in: Carchi, Esmeraldas, Imbabura, Napo, Tungurahua, Pastaza, Chimborazo, Manabí, Galápagos, Santa Elena, Azuay, Loja, Metropolitan District of Quito and Metropolitan District of Guayaquil.

security.tourNote: Ecuador is a safe destination. Our guide holds ANT certification and all road trips are covered by free accident insurance.
